name: Paul's App Store
subtitle: Apps I built for me, my friends, and my family.
description: A personal app store. Apps here are vibe-coded by Paul (with a lot of help from Claude) and shipped through AltStore. If you trust Paul, you can probably trust the apps.

apps.name: Double Exclam Scan
apps.subtitle: Scan a chess board or scoresheet, analyse on Lichess.
apps.localizedDescription: Photograph a chess board or a handwritten scoresheet — from the camera, Photos, or Files. Review the position on an editable board, scrub through scanned games move by move, then open it straight in Lichess analysis. Scanning is stubbed with test data in this build; the full scanner is coming.

versions.localizedDescription: Scans now carry a per-install device id so each phone's contributions to the training flywheel stay separate.
Board scanning is REAL — the CNN runs on your phone (instant, offline). Review the board, fix any wrong squares, analyse. Corrected scans train the next model version.
Scoresheet scanning is REAL: photos go to the Double Exclam scan API (Sonnet OCR + chess-logic auto-repair). Auto-corrected moves shown in amber. Board scanning still uses test data.
Chess.com analysis now opens in an in-app browser — works around the Chess.com app crashing on FEN deep links.
Scan history: every scan is saved and reopenable (clock icon on home). Fixed white squares behind pieces. 'Play through on Lichess' now loads the whole game with a scrubbable move list.
Proper piece images (Lichess cburnett set). Scanned games are real PGN: open as a playable game on Lichess, or Share PGN to paste anywhere.
Nicer board (tournament colours, outlined pieces, coordinates), Chess.com analysis button, opens the Lichess/Chess.com app when installed.
Initial shell: board & scoresheet scan flows with test data, editable board, game replay, Lichess analysis.
